articles.title: Desktop Apps With deno desktop
articles.description: Deno 2.9 (or the 'canary' build now) can turn JavaScript projects into self-contained apps on macOS, Windows, and Linux. Unlike Electron, you can opt to use the default OS WebView or a bundled Chromium backend, plus you get cross-compilation and automatic support for apps built on frameworks like Next.js and SvelteKit.
articles.author: The Deno Project

articles.title: Babel 8.0 Released: The JavaScript Transpiler
articles.description: It’s been eight years since Babel 7.0, and despite the rise of tools like SWC and Oxc, weekly downloads of the @babel packages have increased 380x since. v8.0 is a modernization release that goes ESM-only and targets ~ES2023 by default (vs ES5 before).
articles.author: Babel Team

articles.title: TypeScript 7.0 Release Candidate
articles.description: A step closer for the Go-powered TypeScript compiler that promises ~10x faster build performance. (I've seen people on social media getting very excited by this.)
articles.author: Daniel Rosenwasser (Microsoft)

articles.title: How an Underrated Refactor Saved 90% Memory Usage
articles.description: The tale of how TanStack Table v9 uses less memory than v8 on large tables, thanks to a simple idea: storing methods on shared prototypes instead of creating them for every object. Benchmarks included, plus a pattern other libraries can copy.
articles.author: Kevin Van Cott (TanStack)

articles.title: FullCalendar 7.0: A Full Sized JavaScript Calendar
articles.description: A Google Calendar-style experience for your own apps. Works with React, Vue and Angular (v7.0 adds Angular 22 support), but can be used with plain JavaScript. Here’s a demo where you can play with the themes and styling approaches. MIT licensed with commercial extensions.
articles.author: FullCalendar LLC

articles.title: 🤖 Eve: Like Next.js, But for Building Agents
articles.description: A new framework from Vercel that provides Next.js-esque structure for building AI-powered agents using TypeScript and Markdown.
articles.author: Vercel

articles.title: ForesightJS 4: Predictive User Intent Library for the Web
articles.description: Predicts intent from pointer and keyboard cues to prefetch content before it's needed for a snappier user experience. v4.0 adds official React and Vue packages with a declarative <Foresight> component in v4.2.
articles.author: ForesightJS, Inc.

articles.title: Prop For That: What JS Knows, Now CSS Knows
articles.description: Provides CSS with access to values you'd often need JS to provide (e.g. pointer position, time, a slider’s value). Add attributes to HTML and live values show up as custom properties, bridging the JS–CSS gap. The demo page is neat.
articles.author: Adam Argyle

articles.title: Kage: Shadow a Website for Offline Viewing
articles.description: A tool to clone a site and serve it up for offline viewing. The difference vs "Save As" is it handles JS-rendered sites by saving the DOM using a headless browser. Repo.
articles.author: Duc-Tam Nguyen

articles.title: goja: An ECMAScript/JavaScript Engine in Pure Go
articles.description: A mature JavaScript engine for adding scripting functionality to Go apps without CGo/V8.
articles.author: Dmitry Panov

articles.description: Cloudflare has added the ability to create and deploy Workers on 'temporary accounts'. Ostensibly 'for AI agents', but you can use them yourself too, and get a Worker running for 60 minutes with no strings attached.
TC39 delegate Luca Casonato has made a proposal to add built-in localized time formatting to <time>, akin to GitHub's <relative-time> element.
🤖 MDN has introduced an official MCP server to provide better access to MDN's up-to-date JS and frontend docs to coding agents.
The Bun team has proposed adding shared-memory threads to JavaScriptCore (a more traditional threading approach versus worker threads).
⭐ Vite 8.1 – Adds experimental support for 'bundled dev mode' which radically speeds up dev server startup and full reloads on large apps. Also adds WASM/ESM integration support.

⭐ Astro 7.0 – The popular web framework gets faster build times, with .astro compilation and Markdown/MDX processing going through a Rust-powered pipeline. Advanced Routing also gives you full control over the request pipeline.
pnpm 11.7 and 11.8 – pnpm install gets a --dry-run option, --frozen-store adds support for read-only package stores, and more.
Node.js v26.3.1 (Current), v24.17.0 (LTS), and v22.23.0 (LTS)

articles.title: Modern CSS Theming with light-dark(), contrast-color(), and Style Queries
articles.description: Get dark mode, automatic readable text, and per-component palettes, all from a single brand color with no JavaScript. Una combines light-dark(), the new contrast-color(), and style queries into one adaptive system, now Baseline across browsers.
articles.author: Una Kravets

articles.title: 🔒 PACT: Anonymous Credentials for the Web
articles.description: Mozilla shares the technical details behind their PACT proposal — a privacy-preserving way to curb bot abuse. It aims to let sites rate-limit bots via cryptography, without the need to track users or tie access to your device.
articles.author: Dennis Jackson

articles.title: Astro 7.0 Now Available
articles.description: The popular framework for content-driven sites goes all-in on speed: a new Rust-powered compiler, a Rust Markdown/MDX pipeline, and Vite 8's Rolldown bundler combine for builds 15–61% faster.
articles.author: Phillips, Stoppa, Kane (Astro)

articles.title: Your Grid Lanes Will Likely Fail WCAG 2.4.3
articles.description: A warning on the accessibility pitfalls of the new Grid Lanes feature. Manuel notes that it inherently breaks tab order, and while flow-tolerance can help, its default value is too low and even a high value is no silver bullet.
articles.author: Manuel Matuzovic

articles.title: Animating the <dialog> Element Using View Transitions
articles.description: Rik quickly explains how to animate the <dialog> element using the View Transition API, takes a look at the Invoker Commands API, and shares some tips to improve the default behavior of the <dialog> modal.
articles.author: Rik Schennink

articles.title: Can Your AI Pass the Accessibility Test?
articles.description: Aaron, along with Jessie Lorenz and Carie Fisher, recently gave a talk at Microsoft Build highlighting where coding agents fall short in regards to accessibility: "Out of the box, most coding agents are pretty terrible when it comes to accessibility."
articles.author: Aaron Gustafson

articles.title: Improving Card Patterns with Anchor Positioning
articles.description: Shares a pattern using CSS anchor positioning to solve some of the problems often found with clickable card surfaces.
articles.author: Emil Björklund

articles.title: billboard.js 4.0: A JavaScript Chart Library Based on D3
articles.description: At nine years old, billboard.js now has a new, opt-in canvas-based rendering mode (SVG remains the default), smaller bundles, and better performance on large datasets. There are hundreds of demos ranging from donut charts and funnel charts to overlapping bar charts and radar charts.
articles.author: Jae Sung Park (NAVER)

articles.title: Kage: Shadow a Website for Offline Viewing
articles.description: A tool that clones a web site and serves it up for offline viewing. The difference vs a normal "Save As" is it handles JS-rendered sites by saving the DOM from a headless browser.
articles.author: Duc-Tam Nguyen

articles.title: Wely: A Lightweight Web Component Framework
articles.description: A unified toolkit for building components without a heavy framework. One CLI handles scaffolding, styling, testing, and bundling, and the output is portable native Web Components with a runtime of ~13 KB (Lit internally, plus Tailwind).
articles.author: litepacks

articles.title: Wirewiki: A Resource to Explore Infrastructure for Any Domain
articles.description: Has a simple UI to do all sorts of common lookups including info on hosting, DNS, SPF, A, CNAME, TXT records, MX records, and lots more.
articles.author: Ruurtjan Pul

articles.title: Precise Type: A Type Scale Tool to Craft Precise Typography
articles.description: A full-featured type scale tool to interactively customize modular or metric-based type scales. Also includes info on the models behind font size, line height, and letter spacing. Exports to CSS and CSV.
articles.author: Adonis Raul Raduca

articles.title: Able Player v5.0.0 Released
articles.description: An accessible, cross-browser HTML5 media player (audio and video) designed to enhance the usability of audiovisual content for all users, including those with disabilities. Homepage.
articles.author: Joe Dolson

articles.title: 🎨 Palette Inspiration: Color Palettes Extracted from Famous Art
articles.description: Want a palette inspired by a masterpiece (like these Picassos)? Search and explore palettes built from 23,000+ works of art in different styles and genres.
articles.author: Palette Inspiration

articles.title: 👹 cssQuake: A Port Powered by PolyCSS
articles.description: We featured PolyCSS a few issues ago, and now someone has put it to work to create a CSS-powered Quake. The world is rendered as real HTML/CSS 3D geometry, without a WebGL or canvas renderer. Impressive stuff. You can see the repo here.
articles.author: Agustin Capeletto

articles.title: taken
articles.description: This page reveals what your browser shares the moment you arrive on a site. No login, no form, no permissions. An interesting overview of the data points that are just offered up by default.
articles.author: Rise Up Labs
